CITY MEDICAL CENTER
DEPARTMENT OF RADIOLOGY
123 Medical Plaza, Springfield, IL 62704
Phone: (555) 123-4567

Patient Name: John S. Doe
Patient ID: CMC-45812
Date of Birth: 04/15/2009 (17 years old)

Referring Physician: Dr. Sarah Jenkins, MD
Date of Exam: 10/26/2023
Accession Number: A897654

MRI BRAIN AND CERVICAL SPINE W/O CONTRAST

History/Indication: A 17-year-old male with persistent, throbbing frontal headache and intermittent neck pain, onset 3 weeks prior. No history of trauma.

Comparison: None available.

Findings:

Brain MRI:
Technique: Multiplanar, multisequence imaging of the brain without intravenous contrast.
Parenchyma: The cerebral hemispheres, cerebellum, and brainstem appear unremarkable. No areas of restricted diffusion to suggest acute infarction. T2 and FLAIR sequences show no abnormal signal intensity.

Ventricular System: The ventricles and basilar cisterns are appropriate in size and configuration.

Vasculature: No flow voids or prominent vascular anomalies are seen on this non-contrast study.

Orbits and Paranasal Sinuses: The globes are unremarkable. Mucosal thickening is noted in the ethmoid and maxillary sinuses bilateral, consistent with mild sinusitis.

Cervical Spine MRI:
Technique: Multiplanar, multisequence imaging of the cervical spine without intravenous contrast.
Alignment: Normal cervical lordosis is preserved. No subluxation.

Vertebral Bodies: Vertebral body heights are maintained. No marrow signal abnormality.

Disc Spaces: There is mild disc desiccation noted at C5-C6 and C6-C7. No prominent disc herniation is visualized.

Spinal Canal and Cord: The spinal canal is widely patent. The cervical spinal cord shows normal signal intensity throughout. No evidence of cord compression or syringomyelia.

Paraspinal Tissues: Unremarkable.

Impression:

1. Mild disc desiccation at C5-C6 and C6-C7, without severe stenosis or cord compression.
2. Normal MRI of the brain, except for mild ethmoid and maxillary sinusitis.

Dr. Alan B. Foster, MD, DABR
